name: Sierra Sálvada Loop Trail
name_original: Sierra Sálvada — circular desde Lastras de Teza por la Cima de Moscadero
description: This moderate 18.9-mile mountain bike route from Lastras de Teza takes you to the Moscadero Summit in Sierra Sálvada.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valle De Losa offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region is characterized by its valleys, such as the Valle de Mena, and surrounding mountainous terrain, including the Sierra Sálvada. Riders can expect a mix of elevations, from valley floors to higher summits like Ungino, providing varied challenges. The area features a network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Valle De Losa The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Ordunte - circular route through the Valle de Mena, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Valle de Mena. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra Sálvada Loop Trail, a moderate 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through the Sierra Sálvada mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the Tres Altos — circular desde Villaluenga, a 30.3 miles (48.8 km) trail leading through high points and valleys, often completed in about 4 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Valle De Losa is defined by its valleys, mountain ranges, and varied elevations. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails as well as more advanced mountain bike trails. The routes in Valle De Losa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Losa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Valle De Losa?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails recorded in Valle De Losa on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Valle De Losa?
A: Yes, Valle De Losa offers several easy mountain bike trails. There are 6 routes specifically categorized as easy, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the region. These trails are generally less demanding in terms of elevation and technical skill.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100752270/embalse-de-ordunte-circular-route-through-the-valle-de-mena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Ordunte - circular route through the Valle de Mena&lt;/a&gt; is about 20.4 miles (32.8 km) and takes around 3 hours. Longer routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100845345/tres-altos-circular-desde-villaluenga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Altos — circular desde Villaluenga&lt;/a&gt; stretch to 30.3 miles (48.8 km) and can take over 4.5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Valle De Losa?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Valle De Losa features 27 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes often include significant elevation changes and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100845345/tres-altos-circular-desde-villaluenga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Altos — circular desde Villaluenga&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge – Lookout loop from Valle de Losa&lt;/a&gt;, which involve substantial climbs and descents.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Valle De Losa?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Valle De Losa are designed as circular or loop trails, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100752270/embalse-de-ordunte-circular-route-through-the-valle-de-mena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Ordunte - circular route through the Valle de Mena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103209135/sierra-salvada-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Sálvada Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Valle De Losa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, which includes scenic valleys like Valle de Mena and the challenging terrain of the Sierra Sálvada, offering varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa?
A: While mountain biking in Valle De Losa, you can encounter various natural attractions. The region is known for its valleys and mountainous terrain. You might pass by significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3921708/mountain-passes-around-valle-de-losa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;mountain passes&lt;/a&gt; such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5461431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Ro&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4345740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orduña Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are several &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3815523/lakes-around-valle-de-losa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;lakes and reservoirs&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1353866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maroño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5461432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arceniega Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which often feature in routes.

Q: Are there any hidden gem mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa that are less frequented?
A: While many trails are popular, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ungino Summit and Rock Arch – Ungino Spring loop from Gorobel mendilerroa BBE&lt;/a&gt; offer a more secluded experience. This difficult route takes you through high points and offers stunning views, often with fewer riders than the more well-known paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valle De Losa?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Valle De Losa is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder, with pleasant temperatures and less extreme heat or cold, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: What should I wear for mountain biking in Valle De Losa?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for elevation changes, it's advisable to wear layered clothing that can be adjusted to changing conditions. Technical fabrics that wick away moisture are recommended. Always include a helmet, gloves, and appropriate footwear. Depending on the season, consider bringing a lightweight waterproof jacket and sun protection.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking on trails in Valle De Losa?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on the established trails in Valle De Losa. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations, especially if you plan to ride through protected natural areas or private land. Always respect signage and local guidelines.

Q: Are there options for food and drink along the mountain bike trails in Valle De Losa?
A: While some routes might pass through or near villages with cafes or restaurants, many trails in Valle De Losa traverse natural and rural areas where amenities are scarce. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer rides. Plan your route in advance to identify any potential stops for refreshments.
tips: Summit located at an altitude of 1,078 meters on the border between Casitlla y León and the Basque Country, in the Sierra Sálvada. From the top you can see the characteristic relief of the mountain range with a very peculiar structure created by the steep slopes of the north.

Moscadero (1,132 m.) is located on the border between the Basque Country and the province of Burgos. The top offers impressive panoramic views of the region and the natural limestone wall that closes the Aiara valley.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and required fitness for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, spanning 30.5 kilometers with approximately 590 meters of ascent. It demands good fitness and advanced riding skills, offering a balance of physical challenge and technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the Sierra Sálvada mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain mix. You'll ride on significant portions of loose surfaces (20.0 km), singletrack (4.75 km), natural paths (3.19 km), paved paths (4.17 km), and asphalt (3.06 km). Expect dynamic transitions between surfaces, including features like high prairies, sinkholes, and areas where water disappears underground due to the region's karst geology. Some sections may also require navigating through fences for cattle management.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers panoramic views over valleys, and on clear days, you might even see the Cantabrian Sea. Key points include the Mojón Alto and the Moscadero Summit. The landscape features striking limestone formations, beech forests, and grasslands, with a dramatic 600-meter high and 27-kilometer long limestone wall.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail from Lastras de Teza?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for Lastras de Teza are not provided, but as a circular route starting from the village, you would typically look for available public parking within Lastras de Teza or at a designated trailhead parking area nearby.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to Lastras de Teza?
faqs.answer: Information on public transport directly to Lastras de Teza is not detailed. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport options if you plan to access the trailhead without a private vehicle.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not provided. Given the presence of cattle and the area being a Special Protection Area (SPA) for birds, it is recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-controlled to protect wildlife and livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Sálvada region experiences diverse conditions due to its location between Atlantic and Mediterranean climates. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions for mountain biking. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in Sierra Sálvada?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Sálvada is designated as a Special Protection Area (SPA) for birds. While specific permits for mountain biking are not mentioned, it's always wise to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife. There are no entrance fees for the general area.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or accommodation near Lastras de Teza?
faqs.answer: Lastras de Teza is a small village. For cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you may find limited options directly in Lastras de Teza itself. It is recommended to check nearby larger towns or plan your amenities before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, Artzaintza naturbidea, Sopeñako errege bidea, Euskal Herriko ur-mugaren bidea, and Calle Mediavilla.

faqs.question: What makes the Sierra Sálvada region unique for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Sálvada is unique for its dramatic geological formations, including a 25 km long steep north slope and a 600-meter high limestone wall. Its position as a natural boundary between Atlantic and Mediterranean climates contributes to rich biodiversity, making for a scenic and ecologically interesting ride.
